title: Luetkea cinerascens
intro: Luetkea cinerascens, also known as Grey-leaved Alpine, is a small, perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. It has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, with health benefits attributed to its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Luetkea cinerascens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Luetkea cinerascens is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Luetkea cinerascens is classified within the family Rosaceae. This classification places it among the rose family, which includes other flowering plants such as roses, apples, and pears. The genus Luetkea is a small group of plants native to North America, specifically the western regions. Luetkea cinerascens, also known as silvertop, is a low-growing, woody shrub with silvery-gray foliage. Within the Rosaceae family, Luetkea cinerascens is further classified into the tribe Amygdaleae, a group of plants including almonds and cherry species.
